title: The Agent stages a journal entry (or three journal entries) in fiscal period brookfield_FP-2026-05 totaling $147,825 across three service-line revenue credit lines with an offsetting debit to account 119000, prepared by george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com, and submits it for approval (without approving or posting it).
justification: The prompt explicitly instructs: 'stage the recognition entry for the $147,825 across the three service lines so it is queued for Daniel's review through the normal close path.' Per OE22, staging stops at submitted because Daniel is the next-up reviewer; approving or posting would jump the close path.
evidence: Look for an oracle_gl_create_journal_entry call with period_id brookfield_FP-2026-05, prepared_by george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com, and lines totaling $147,825 across three service-line revenue accounts (credits) with an offsetting debit to account_number 119000 (Work in Process - Unbilled Services on brookfield). Confirm an oracle_gl_submit_journal_entry call follows on the same entry_id and that there is no oracle_gl_approve_journal_entry or oracle_gl_post_journal_entry call on the entry. A single consolidated entry or three separate entries summing to $147,825 are both acceptable as long as the total ties out and each lands in the submitted (review) queue.

title: The Agent's staged journal entry carries a business justification referencing the May engagement-stage completion review from Andrea Phil and exception exc_1ddfc978ce5a4d (or similar).
justification: Per OE22, the JE must be staged with a business justification grounding it in Andrea's May engagement-stage completion review and the related accept-timing disposition on exc_1ddfc978ce5a4d. This is how the entry stands on its own at Daniel's review and ties back to the package memo.
evidence: Check the business_justification (or equivalent description/memo) parameter on the oracle_gl_create_journal_entry call. It must reference the May engagement-stage completion review (or Andrea Phil's stage-completion sign-off) and the related exception exc_1ddfc978ce5a4d on the BL-75810CD0FEE4 disposition.

title: The Agent adds an accept-timing variance explanation entry to reconciliation BL-75810CD0FEE4, leaving the existing partial-feed variance explanation in place and without submitting, approving, or certifying the reconciliation.
justification: The prompt says: 'Update the reconciliation and exception dispositions to accept-timing per Hannah, leaving the underlying exception trail referenced as-is for now.' Per OE23, the recon disposition is updated through a new variance_explanation entry rather than progressing the recon state, because the underlying trail is to be left as-is until the June rollforward lands.
evidence: Look for a blackline_update_reconciliation_variances call with recon_id BL-75810CD0FEE4 that adds a new variance_explanation entry capturing the accept-timing disposition. Confirm the call does not strip or rewrite the existing partial-feed explanation. Confirm there is no blackline_submit_reconciliation, blackline_approve_reconciliation, or blackline_certify_reconciliation call on this recon.

title: The Agent's recon update on BL-75810CD0FEE4 references Hannah Grant's 2026-06-02 accept-timing approval, the FP-2026-06 BD3 revisit, and exception exc_1ddfc978ce5a4d (or similar).
justification: Per OE23, the accept-timing disposition entry has to identify the approver (Hannah Grant), the date of approval (2026-06-02), the revisit anchor (FP-2026-06 BD3), and the linked exception (exc_1ddfc978ce5a4d), so the disposition trail is self-contained when Daniel reviews it.
evidence: Check the variance_explanation entry added by the blackline_update_reconciliation_variances call on BL-75810CD0FEE4. The text should reference Hannah Grant's accept-timing approval (June 2, 2026 or similar wording), the FP-2026-06 BD3 revisit hook, and the related exception exc_1ddfc978ce5a4d.

title: The Agent updates exception exc_1ddfc978ce5a4d to record an accept-timing disposition without resolving the exception, leaving its state as investigating with resolution_summary and resolution_executed_at unset.
justification: The prompt explicitly says: 'leaving the underlying exception trail referenced as-is for now.' Per OE24 and Hannah Grant's reply email_scen_010_orphan_exception_0009, the underlying exception trail stays referenced as-is until FP-2026-06 BD3, so the state must remain investigating and the resolution fields must stay empty.
evidence: Look for a blackline_update_exception call with exception_id exc_1ddfc978ce5a4d that records the accept-timing disposition (e.g., in proposed_resolution or a disposition note field). Confirm there is no blackline_resolve_exception call on this exception_id and that resolution_summary / resolution_executed_at remain unset (state remains investigating).

title: The Agent's exception update on exc_1ddfc978ce5a4d references Hannah Grant's 2026-06-02 accept-timing approval and the FP-2026-06 BD3 revisit (or similar).
justification: Per OE24, the disposition update must make the accept-timing decision and its revisit anchor traceable on the exception itself, not just on the recon, so the audit trail is complete on both sides.
evidence: Check the proposed_resolution (or disposition note field) on the blackline_update_exception call for exc_1ddfc978ce5a4d. The text should reference Hannah Grant's accept-timing approval, the date 2026-06-02 (or June 2, 2026 or similar), and the FP-2026-06 BD3 revisit.

title: The Agent uploads a supporting memo to the Records Vault for the May Brookfield WIP-to-revenue recognition package with classification restricted and retention_policy_code AICPA_SQMS_7Y, linked to the staged journal entry.
justification: The prompt instructs: 'File the supporting memo and stage schedule in the vault under the right firm classification and retention.' Per OE20 and OE25, the firm SQMS retention for journal-entry support is AICPA_SQMS_7Y and the existing reference doc on this exact subject (doc_42c851aed8fb40ab) is filed as restricted; the new memo mirrors both values.
evidence: Look for a records_vault_upload_document call with kind journal_entry_support (or memo), classification restricted, retention_policy_code AICPA_SQMS_7Y, related_resource_type journal_entry, and related_resource_id pointing to the staged JE entry_id from the earlier oracle_gl_create_journal_entry call. Title should reference the FP-2026-05 WIP recognition package / stage schedule.

title: The Agent's uploaded memo covers the brookfield_time_and_wip_feed subledger feed run for FP-2026-05 (run_e33ed2561f2c46) showing status success with 2083 of 2083 rows posted and 0 rejected, flagged as a structural contradiction to the partial-feed narrative recorded on the recon (or similar).
justification: The prompt explicitly says: 'The vault memo needs to be complete, not a paraphrase of what Anaya remembers.' The load-bearing structural truth (per OE14 and OE15) is the feed-run record showing a clean, fully posted batch. The memo must surface that read so the package can stand on its own when Daniel opens it.
evidence: Check the content_b64 (decoded) of the records_vault_upload_document call. The memo body should reference the feed run identifier (run_e33ed2561f2c46) or the feed name brookfield_time_and_wip_feed for brookfield_FP-2026-05 with the status success / 2083 rows posted / 0 rejected facts, and call out that this does not match the partial-feed narrative carried on the BL-75810CD0FEE4 variance explanation.

title: The Agent posts a reply in the #monthly-close-coordination Slack channel (channel_id C005) on the existing triage thread (thread_ts 1780248600.000000).
justification: The prompt says: 'Drop a brief note in the close coordination channel on the thread we already have running.' Per OE11 and OE26, the existing running thread is parented by ts 1780248600.000000 in channel C005, so the note belongs as a reply on that thread rather than as a new top-level message.
evidence: Look for a slack_conversations_add_message call with channel_id C005 (or channel name monthly-close-coordination) and thread_ts 1780248600.000000. Confirm the tool returned a success response.

title: The Agent's Slack reply in #monthly-close-coordination notes that the $147,825 May recognition is staged for Daniel Jones and the accept-timing disposition is applied on BL-75810CD0FEE4 and exc_1ddfc978ce5a4d per Hannah Grant (or similar).
justification: Per OE26, the channel note has to give the running close-coordination triage thread the same read the package memo carries to Daniel, anchored on the staged recognition figure and the dispositions.
evidence: Check the payload parameter of the slack_conversations_add_message call to C005 thread 1780248600.000000. It must reference the $147,825 May recognition being staged (or queued for Daniel) and the accept-timing dispositions on BL-75810CD0FEE4 and exc_1ddfc978ce5a4d per Hannah Grant.

title: The Agent sends an email reply on email_scen_059_wip_recognition_0000 from george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com to andrea.phil@brookfieldcpas.com with margaret.sullivan@brookfieldcpas.com in CC.
justification: The prompt instructs: 'reply to Andrea with the recognition status and confirmation that the residual is parked per her instruction.' Per OE27, the reply preserves Margaret Sullivan's cc from the original stage-completion email so she keeps visibility before the recognition entry is booked.
evidence: Look for an email_reply_to_email call with email_id email_scen_059_wip_recognition_0000, sender george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com, and cc containing margaret.sullivan@brookfieldcpas.com. The reply target andrea.phil@brookfieldcpas.com is implicit from the parent email's sender.

title: The Agent's email reply to Andrea Phil confirms the $147,825 May recognition is staged across the three service lines and queued for Daniel Jones's review (or similar).
justification: The prompt says: 'reply to Andrea with the recognition status.' Per OE27, the reply must close the loop on the recognition figure Andrea sent over by confirming the staged total and that it is now in Daniel's queue.
evidence: Check the content parameter of the email_reply_to_email call to email_scen_059_wip_recognition_0000. It must state that the $147,825 May recognition is staged across the three service lines and queued for Daniel Jones / through the normal close path.

title: The Agent's email reply to Andrea Phil confirms the $4,390.62 residual on BL-75810CD0FEE4 is parked for next-period cleanup per her instruction and the accept-timing disposition is applied per Hannah Grant (or similar).
justification: The prompt explicitly says: 'confirmation that the residual is parked per her instruction' and asks Hannah's accept-timing path be applied. Per OE27, the reply ties Andrea's leave-the-residual ask to the accept-timing trail Hannah approved.
evidence: Check the content parameter of the email_reply_to_email call to email_scen_059_wip_recognition_0000. It must reference the $4,390.62 residual on BL-75810CD0FEE4 being parked for next-period cleanup per Andrea's instruction and the accept-timing disposition applied per Hannah Grant.

title: The Agent's email reply to Andrea Phil surfaces that the brookfield_time_and_wip_feed subledger feed run for FP-2026-05 (run_e33ed2561f2c46) shows status success with 2083 of 2083 rows posted and 0 rejected, which does not match the partial-success batch narrative carried on the recon (or similar).
justification: The prompt says: 'If anything in what you pull together changes the read on this before I take it to Daniel, say so plainly. I would rather hear it from you before the package moves than from Andrea after.' Per OE27, the feed-run finding is the single read-change the package surfaces, and George has to flag it in his reply to Andrea before the memo moves up.
evidence: Check the content parameter of the email_reply_to_email call to email_scen_059_wip_recognition_0000. It must reference the brookfield_time_and_wip_feed run for FP-2026-05 (or run_e33ed2561f2c46) with the status success / 2083 rows posted / 0 rejected facts and flag the mismatch against the partial-feed narrative.

title: The Agent deletes the open orphan-exception reminder reminder_scen_010_orphan_exception_0000.
justification: The prompt explicitly says: 'Close the open reminder on the orphan exception once the disposition is updated.' Per OE21 and OE28, this is the reminder tied to exc_1ddfc978ce5a4d on BL-75810CD0FEE4, and it is closed by deleting it once the dispositions are applied.
evidence: Look for a reminder_delete_reminder call with reminder_id reminder_scen_010_orphan_exception_0000. Confirm the tool returned a success response.

title: The Agent creates a new follow-up reminder due on or around 2026-07-03 for the FP-2026-06 BD3 revisit on exc_1ddfc978ce5a4d / BL-75810CD0FEE4 referencing Hannah Grant's accept-timing approval (or similar).
justification: The prompt explicitly says: 'set a fresh follow-up for the FP-2026-06 BD3 revisit Hannah called for so it does not slip next month.' Per OE13 (fiscal period record) and OE29, the FP-2026-06 bd3_lock_at is 2026-07-03, so the reminder anchors there.
evidence: Look for a reminder_add_reminder call with a due_datetime on or around 2026-07-03 (the FP-2026-06 BD3 anchor) and a title or description referencing the FP-2026-06 BD3 revisit on exc_1ddfc978ce5a4d and/or BL-75810CD0FEE4 with Hannah Grant's accept-timing approval / the engagement-hours rollforward check.

title: The Agent reports that the brookfield_time_and_wip_feed subledger feed run for FP-2026-05 (run_e33ed2561f2c46) shows status success with 2083 of 2083 rows posted and 0 rejected, which does not match the partial-success batch narrative carried on the BL-75810CD0FEE4 variance explanation and the persona's recollection (or similar).
justification: This is the single hardest discriminating criterion in the package: the structural feed-run record directly contradicts the partial-feed narrative repeated across the recon, the doppelganger, Andrea's email, the Slack thread, the messaging DM, and George's prior recommendation email. The prompt's escape-valve clause ('If anything in what you pull together changes the read on this before I take it to Daniel, say so plainly') is built around exactly this finding.
evidence: Check the agent's final response for a statement that the brookfield_time_and_wip_feed run for FP-2026-05 (or run_e33ed2561f2c46) is status success with 2083 of 2083 rows posted and 0 rejected, contrasted with the partial-feed narrative on the recon. A response that recites the partial-success batch story without flagging this contradiction fails the rubric.

title: The Agent flags the open review note rn_564e65ce0d594f on reconciliation BL-75810CD0FEE4 (Edith Banda's foreign-currency revaluation question with sla_due_at past relative to today) as an unresolved reviewer item that must be acknowledged in the package rather than auto-cleared (or similar).
justification: The prompt explicitly asks for 'anything else opened or reviewed against it that I have not seen including any open reviewer notes.' Per OE7, Edith Banda's review note rn_564e65ce0d594f is open, its SLA (2026-06-02) is already past, and it has no response or cleared_at; the package must acknowledge it rather than auto-clear it.
evidence: Check the agent's final response, the memo body, and/or the email reply for a statement that reviews note rn_564e65ce0d594f authored by Edith Banda on BL-75810CD0FEE4 is an open foreign-currency revaluation question that is past its SLA and remains outstanding. A response that omits this review note or treats it as cleared fails.

title: The Agent identifies that the parallel reconciliation record blackline_bdbbea5db590 carries the same brookfield_FP-2026-05 period, account 119000, anaya.wallace@brookfieldcpas.com preparer, and $4,390.62 variance as BL-75810CD0FEE4 and is a duplicate scaffold of the same incident, not a second variance or additional exposure (or similar).
justification: Per OE3 through OE5, the two recons share identical entity, period, account, preparer, variance, and balances; only the variance_explanations wording differs. The canonical anchor named by ID in the persona context is BL-75810CD0FEE4. The package must call out the doppelganger as a parallel scaffold rather than aggregate or substitute it.
evidence: Check the agent's final response, the memo body, and/or the email reply for a statement that blackline_bdbbea5db590 is a parallel scaffold of BL-75810CD0FEE4 covering the same $4,390.62 incident (same entity, period brookfield_FP-2026-05, account 119000, preparer anaya.wallace@brookfieldcpas.com, variance), and is not aggregated as a second item. A response that reports both records as separate variances (e.g., totaling ~$8,781.24) or that picks only the doppelganger and never names BL-75810CD0FEE4 as the canonical anchor fails.

title: The Agent reports the current state of reconciliation BL-75810CD0FEE4 as in_progress with a $4,390.62 variance, the partial-feed variance explanation attributed to anaya.wallace@brookfieldcpas.com, no attachments, no reviewer assigned, and no submitted, approved, or certified timestamps (or similar).
justification: The prompt asks for 'the actual state, the evidence attached, anything else opened or reviewed against it that I have not seen.' Per OE4 and OE6, the canonical recon record shows in_progress, $4,390.62 variance, the partial-feed explanation by Anaya, attachments empty, no reviewer, and no later-state timestamps. The package must report the actual state rather than infer progression.
evidence: Check the agent's final response (and the memo body) for a description of BL-75810CD0FEE4's current state matching the canonical record: in_progress, $4,390.62 variance on the partial-feed explanation by Anaya Wallace, attachments empty, no reviewer assigned, and no submitted_at / approved_at / certified_at / archived_at timestamps. A response that asserts the recon has been submitted, certified, reviewed, or that there are evidence attachments, fails.
